The constraints are up to 1000 rounds. So a brute-force approach is impossible. So dynamic programming seems necessary.

private int getContribution(char aMove, char bMove) {
    if ((bMove == 'W' && aMove == 'F') || 
        (bMove == 'E' && aMove == 'W') || 
        (bMove == 'F' && aMove == 'E')) {
        return 1; // Bob gains 1
    } else if ((aMove == 'W' && bMove == 'F') || 
               (aMove == 'E' && bMove == 'W') || 
               (aMove == 'F' && bMove == 'E')) {
        return -1; // Bob loses 1 (Alice gains 1)
    } else {
        return 0; // same move
    }
}

Now, the code:

public int countWinningSequences(String s) {
    int MOD = 1000000007;
    int n = s.length();
    if (n == 0) return 0;
    
    // For each step, we have a 3 x 2001 array
    int[][] prevDp = new int[3][2001];
    int[][] currDp = new int[3][2001];
    
    // Initialize for the first round (i=0)
    char firstA = s.charAt(0);
    for (int move = 0; move < 3; move++) {
        char bobMove = getCharFromMove(move);
        int contribution = getContribution(firstA, bobMove);
        int d = contribution;
        int index = d + 1000;
        prevDp[move][index] += 1;
    }
    
    for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {
        char aMove = s.charAt(i);
        // For each previous move (prevMove)
        for (int prevMove = 0; prevMove < 3; prevMove++) {
            // For each possible difference in prevDp[prevMove]
            for (int idx = 0; idx < 2001; idx++) {
                if (prevDp[prevMove][idx] == 0) continue;
                int prevDiff = idx - 1000;
                // For each possible currentMove not equal to prevMove
                for (int currentMove = 0; currentMove < 3; currentMove++) {
                    if (currentMove == prevMove) continue;
                    char bobMove = getCharFromMove(currentMove);
                    int contribution = getContribution(aMove, bobMove);
                    int newDiff = prevDiff + contribution;
                    int newIdx = newDiff + 1000;
                    currDp[currentMove][newIdx] = (currDp[currentMove][newIdx] + prevDp[prevMove][idx]) % MOD;
                }
            }
        }
        // Swap prevDp and currDp for next iteration
        int[][] temp = prevDp;
        prevDp = currDp;
        currDp = temp;
        // Reset currDp for next step
        for (int m = 0; m < 3; m++) {
            for (int j = 0; j < 2001; j++) {
                currDp[m][j] = 0;
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Sum all cases where difference > 0
    long result = 0;
    for (int move = 0; move < 3; move++) {
        for (int idx = 0; idx < 2001; idx++) {
            int d = idx - 1000;
            if (d > 0) {
                result = (result + prevDp[move][idx]) % MOD;
            }
        }
    }
    return (int) result;
}

Then, for the next step, we need to reset currDp (which is now the previous step's data) to zero, because in the next iteration, currDp will be used to accumulate the next step's data.

But this approach of resetting currDp after each step is O(3*2001) per step, which is acceptable.

But in Java, when you assign temp = prevDp; prevDp = currDp; currDp = temp; then temp is the old prevDp. Then, when you reset currDp (which is now temp, the old prevDp), you zero out the array. That's correct.

But for the first step, after processing i=1, prevDp is the currDp (the data for i=1), and currDp is the previous prevDp (i=0's data), which is then reset. So for the next step, when processing i=2, currDp is the reset array, and we can accumulate into it.

public int countWinningSequences(String s) {
    int MOD = 1000000007;
    int n = s.length();
    if (n == 0) return 0;
    
    // For each step, we have a 3 x 2001 array
    int[][] prevDp = new int[3][2001];
    int[][] currDp = new int[3][2001];
    
    // Initialize for the first round (i=0)
    char firstA = s.charAt(0);
    for (int move = 0; move < 3; move++) {
        char bobMove = getCharFromMove(move);
        int contribution = getContribution(firstA, bobMove);
        int d = contribution;
        int index = d + 1000;
        prevDp[move][index] += 1;
    }
    
    for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {
        char aMove = s.charAt(i);
        // For each previous move (prevMove)
        for (int prevMove = 0; prevMove < 3; prevMove++) {
            // For each possible difference in prevDp[prevMove]
            for (int idx = 0; idx < 2001; idx++) {
                if (prevDp[prevMove][idx] == 0) continue;
                int prevDiff = idx - 1000;
                // For each possible currentMove not equal to prevMove
                for (int currentMove = 0; currentMove < 3; currentMove++) {
                    if (currentMove == prevMove) continue;
                    char bobMove = getCharFromMove(currentMove);
                    int contribution = getContribution(aMove, bobMove);
                    int newDiff = prevDiff + contribution;
                    int newIdx = newDiff + 1000;
                    currDp[currentMove][newIdx] = (currDp[currentMove][newIdx] + prevDp[prevMove][idx]) % MOD;
                }
            }
        }
        // Swap prevDp and currDp for next iteration
        int[][] temp = prevDp;
        prevDp = currDp;
        currDp = temp;
        // Reset currDp for next step
        for (int m = 0; m < 3; m++) {
            for (int j = 0; j < 2001; j++) {
                currDp[m][j] = 0;
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Sum all cases where difference > 0
    long result = 0;
    for (int move = 0; move < 3; move++) {
        for (int idx = 0; idx < 2001; idx++) {
            int d = idx - 1000;
            if (d > 0) {
                result = (result + prevDp[move][idx]) % MOD;
            }
        }
    }
    return (int) result;
}
